revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Harina-jinja]][[ja:針名神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Harina Shrine | native_name = 針名神社 | image = Harinajinja2.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Harina Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.116416666666666|137.01002777777776}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Harina Shrine'''|針名神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q11470699|ltq=Q11470699|qid=Q11470699|1=Yamada district, Owari}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11465292|ltq=Q11465292|qid=Q11465292|1=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} ** Series ordinal: 109 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039553|ltq=Q135039553|qid=Q135039553|1=Kaharano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Ifuno Shrine|lt=Ifuno Shrine|qid=Q135039555|1=Ifuno Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q1155245|ltq=Q1155245|qid=Q1155245|1=Tenpaku-ku}} * {{ill|Q514506|ltq=Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} * {{ill|Aichi District|lt=Aichi district|ceb|Aichi-gun|de|Aichi-gun|en|Aichi District|es|Distrito de Aichi (Aichi)|fa|شهرستان آیچی، آیچی|fr|District d'Aichi|hu|Aicsi körzet|id|Distrik Aichi, Aichi|it|Distretto di Aichi|ja|愛知郡 (愛知県)|ko|아이치군|pl|Powiat Aichi|ru|Айти (уезд)|tl|Distrito ng Aichi, Aichi|uk|Повіт Айті|vi|Aichi (huyện)|zh|愛知郡 (愛知縣)|qid=Q552687|1=Aichi District}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.116416666666666|137.01002777777776}} == image (P18) == * Harinajinja2.JPG == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181449 ** P3831: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * はりなじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8180005001737 == Commons category (P373) == * Harina-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Harinano Shrine|lt=Harinano Shrine|qid=Q135039554|1=Harinano Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県名古屋市天白区天白町平針大根ケ越175', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県名古屋市天白区天白町大字平針字大根ケ越175', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Harina-jinja}} [[JA:針名神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11648156}} == Japanese content == {{Source citation needed|date=August 27, 2017 (Sun) 02:37 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |Name = Harina Shrine |Image = [[File:Harina Jinja Shrine Haiden, Onegakoshi Hirabari Tempaku-cho Tempaku Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|260px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya City|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Tempaku Ward|Q1155245|qid=Q1155245|1=Tenpaku-ku}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tempaku-chō Ōaza Hirabari|1=UNKNOWN}} Aza Onegakoshi 175 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oji Harina Ne no Muraji no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |Rank = Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} |Founded = Unknown (before the {{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|qid=Q7016|1=17th century}}) |Other names = Tenjin
Hirabari Tenjin |Annual festival = |Rituals = Ōharae |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Harina Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Harina Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Harina Shrine''' (針名神社, Harina-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tempaku-chō Ōaza Hirabari|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Tempaku Ward|Q1155245|qid=Q1155245|1=Tenpaku-ku}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya City|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is traditionally identified with the “Harina Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Aichi District (Aichi Prefecture)|Q552687|qid=Q552687|lt=Aichi District|1=Aichi District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, although, as described below, there are differing theories. === History === Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|qid=Q184963}}, it was called '''Tenjin'''. The early Edo–period document {{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|qid=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}} states, “Hirabari Village … Tenjin … exempt from dues,” suggesting that it was already enshrined as Tenjin by the {{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|qid=Q7016|1=17th century}}. A {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|qid=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} dated {{ill|lt=1699|Q7710|qid=Q7710|1=1699}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 12) remains, inscribed with “Tenjingū.” Since the Edo period, the Murase family (the {{ill|lt=shōya|Q110095960|qid=Q110095960|1=Nanushi}} of Hirabari Village) has served as {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q11589454|qid=Q11589454|1=Shinkan (official)}}. Until the early Edo period, the shrine stood at a place called “Motogō” on the left bank of the {{ill|lt=Tempaku River (Aichi Prefecture)|Q11443111|qid=Q11443111|lt=Tempaku River|1=Tenpaku River}} northwest of the current site. In {{ill|lt=1612|Q6771|qid=Q6771|1=1612}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 17), when Hirabari Village was relocated southward in connection with the improvement of the {{ill|lt=Suruga Kaidō|Q24864271|qid=Q24864271|1=Suruga Kaidō}} by order of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|qid=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, the shrine was also moved to its present location. Place names such as “Kanda” still remain around present-day Hirabari 1-chōme, reflecting the former sacred fields of the shrine. {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|qid=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine with the “Harina Shrine” of Aichi District, Owari Province, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and this view subsequently became the prevailing theory. However, Amano’s basis for the identification was merely that the shrine is located in Hirabari Village of Aichi District and shares the character “hari” (針), and no munafuda bearing the name “Harina” has been found. The late Edo–period {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=local historian|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|qid=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} sharply criticized this identification, arguing that Hirabari Village belonged not to Aichi District but to {{ill|lt=Yamada District (Owari Province)|Q11470699|qid=Q11470699|lt=Yamada District|1=Yamada district, Owari}} prior to the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}. Tsuda asserted that the original “Harina Shrine” was the Tenjin Shrine in {{ill|lt=Karasumori-chō|Q11567254|qid=Q11567254|1=Kasumori-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Nakamura Ward|Q780521|qid=Q780521|1=Nakamura-ku, Nagoya}}. Recent studies have also suggested that the Tempaku Ward area, including Hirabari, likely belonged to Yamada District in the medieval period, indicating that there is room for reconsideration regarding the identification of this shrine as “Harina Shrine”{{Sfn|Nagoya Local Culture Society|1982|pp=23–29}}. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|qid=Q2057|1=1909}} (Meiji 42), a nearby Hachiman shrine was merged, adding {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} to the enshrined deities. The present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine buildings|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine office|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=temizuya|1=UNKNOWN}}, and others were completed on {{ill|lt=November 6|Q2992|qid=Q2992|1=November 6}}, {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|qid=Q2480|1=1976}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 51) as a commemorative project celebrating the 50th anniversary of the enthronement of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōwa|Q34479|qid=Q34479|1=Hirohito}}. The precincts cover approximately 12,000 tsubo, making it one of the largest shrines in {{ill|lt=Nagoya City|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}. In {{ill|lt=1982|Q2436|qid=Q2436|1=1982}} (Shōwa 57), the traditional Ten’ō Festival event “Chōchin Tomoshi” was revived. === Enshrined deities === The principal deity, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oji Harina Ne no Muraji no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, is regarded as an ancestral deity of the Owari clan and the fourteenth-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Amateruhiakari no Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}, who is also enshrined at {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|qid=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}}, the ichinomiya of Owari Province. Together with his father, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Otsuna Ne no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, he is also enshrined at {{ill|lt=Haritsuna Shrine|Q11648178|qid=Q11648178|1=Haritsuna Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Inuyama City|Q503411|qid=Q503411|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}. As associated deities, the shrine enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} (also known as Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) and Sukunahikona no Kami, who is said in the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki myths to have traveled throughout the land assisting in nation-building. {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} was merged and enshrined in {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|qid=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42). === Festivals and rituals === * New Year’s Day Festival ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=New Year’s Day|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Sagichō ({{ill|lt=January 14|Q2257|qid=Q2257|1=January 14}}) * Harina Tenjin Shrine Festival ({{ill|lt=January 25|Q2279|qid=Q2279|1=January 25}}) * Harina Inari Shrine Hatsuuma Festival * National Foundation Festival ({{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|qid=Q2335|1=February 11}}) * Kinen Festival * Ōharae ({{ill|lt=June|Q120|qid=Q120|1=June}} and {{ill|lt=December|Q126|qid=Q126|1=December}}) * {{ill|lt=Ten’ō Festival|Q17221495|qid=Q17221495|1=Gion and Tenno Festivals}} ({{ill|lt=Marine Day|Q1355833|qid=Q1355833|1=Marine Day}}) * Annual Festival ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=Labor Thanksgiving Day|Q82405|qid=Q82405|1=Labor Thanksgiving Day}}) * Tenchō Festival === Gallery === {{gallery |File:Harina Jinja Shrine Shimmon, Onegakoshi Hirabari Tempaku-cho Tempaku Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Shrine gate |File:Harinajinja4.JPG|Shrine grounds }} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture|Q30925254|qid=Q30925254|1=list of Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture}} === External links === {{osm box|w|110166866}} * [http://www.harina3.or.jp/ Harina Shrine] * Prefecture]]
